diagrams.description: This timeline traces the development of historical writing and thought from ancient times to the present. It begins with the first historians: Herodotus and Thucydides in Greece (5th cent. BCE), Sima Qian in China (1st cent. BCE). The Renaissance (15th‑16th c.) revived secular history. The Enlightenment (18th c.) introduced philosophical history (Voltaire, Gibbon). The 19th century saw the rise of professional, scientific history with Leopold von Ranke’s motto “wie es eigentlich gewesen” (1824). Marxist historiography emerged after 1848. In the 20th century, the Annales School (1929) shifted focus to long‑term structures; the linguistic and cultural turns (1970s‑80s) questioned narrative and objectivity; feminist and postcolonial histories reshaped the field. The late 20th and early 21st centuries brought digital history, global history, and the study of memory.
